scouting_reports.strengths: Extraordinary raw pace, fearless overtaking, adaptable in all conditions
scouting_reports.weaknesses: Occasional over-aggression in wheel-to-wheel, young and still maturing
scouting_reports.notes: Antonelli is the generational talent. His Bahrain Feature win was dominant — fastest in every sector. He has the same instinctive speed Verstappen showed in F3.
scouting_reports.projection: F1 race seat 2026 — future WDC contender

scouting_reports.strengths: Brilliant tire management, smooth and consistent, great in traffic
scouting_reports.weaknesses: Qualifying pace sometimes lacking vs top competitors
scouting_reports.notes: Bortoleto is the complete package. His consistency is remarkable — always in the points, rarely makes mistakes. Sprint race win in Bahrain was clinical.

scouting_reports.strengths: Blistering one-lap pace, aggressive but clean overtaking
scouting_reports.weaknesses: Consistency needs work — can have quiet weekends mixed with brilliant ones
scouting_reports.notes: Hadjar is lightning quick. His Saudi sprint win was a masterclass in qualifying-to-race conversion. When he's on it, nobody can touch him.

scouting_reports.strengths: Already has F1 experience (Haas sub), mature beyond his years, consistent
scouting_reports.weaknesses: Needs more dominant wins, qualifying can improve
scouting_reports.notes: Bearman has already tasted F1 and proved he belongs. His Saudi P2 shows he's ready to step up.

scouting_reports.strengths: IndyCar champion-level pace, oval experience unique, aggressive racer
scouting_reports.weaknesses: Super License situation complicated, no single-seater feeder series recently
scouting_reports.notes: Herta is the American F1 dream. His raw speed is undeniable. The question isn't talent — it's logistics and super license.
scouting_reports.projection: F1 test/reserve 2026, race seat if SL resolved

scouting_reports.strengths: Exceptional raw pace for his age, strong starts, fearless wheel-to-wheel
scouting_reports.weaknesses: Still learning tire management at this level, can overdrive
scouting_reports.notes: Lindblad is the future. At 17, he's already dominating F3 qualifying. His Bahrain sprint win was clinical.

scouting_reports.strengths: American talent pipeline, solid racecraft, improving every weekend
scouting_reports.weaknesses: Not yet at the level of top F2 peers, needs more race wins
scouting_reports.notes: Crawford represents the American F1 pipeline. His P4 in Saudi shows improvement. Haas would benefit from an American driver for marketing.

driver_market.reason: Alpine Academy graduate, contract expires end of 2025. Multiple teams interested.
Locked into Mercedes pathway but could be loaned to a customer team.
No current F1 contract. Super license situation the only barrier. American marketing value enormous for Haas.
Sauber Academy but contract situation unclear. Strong F2 results making him attractive.
Ferrari Academy but could be placed at Haas (Ferrari partnership). Already has F1 race experience.
American driver with F2 experience. Perfect marketing fit for Haas US fanbase.

feed_posts.author: Haas neXT
feed_posts.content: RACE RECAP: Antonelli dominates Bahrain Feature Race! The 18-year-old Italian put on a masterclass, leading every lap from pole. This kid is special. Fan Vote is LIVE — should he test with Haas?

feed_posts.author: Alpine neXT
feed_posts.content: DRIVER INTEL: Gabriel Bortoleto's tire management data from Bahrain is extraordinary. His deg numbers are better than some current F1 drivers. Alpine Academy product — could he be France's next F1 star?

feed_posts.author: Haas neXT
feed_posts.content: FAN VOTE UPDATE: Colton Herta leads the Haas neXT vote with 2,340 votes! The American driver has massive fan support. Super License situation remains the key question.

feed_posts.author: Alpine neXT
feed_posts.content: RISE+ CHALLENGE: Scout 5 F3 drivers this weekend and earn 250 RISE+ points! Top scouts get an exclusive invite to the Alpine garage at Silverstone.

feed_posts.author: Haas neXT
feed_posts.content: MARKET ALERT: Oliver Bearman's contract with Ferrari Academy could see him placed at Haas for 2026. The British teenager already has F1 race experience from his Jeddah sub.

feed_posts.author: neXT Feed
feed_posts.content: BREAKING: Arvid Lindblad wins F3 Bahrain Sprint at just 17 years old! Red Bull Junior dominating the feeder series. Remember this name.

feed_posts.author: Alpine neXT
feed_posts.content: RISE+ EVENT: Meet the next gen at Silverstone! RISE+ members get paddock access, driver meet & greets, and pit lane walks during the F2/F3 support races. Points required: 5,000

feed_posts.author: Haas neXT
feed_posts.content: SCOUTING REPORT: Jak Crawford's P4 in Saudi shows steady improvement. The American is exactly what Haas needs for their US market strategy. Full analysis on his profile.
